name: iterative-name-corrections
description: User changes their mind once, then changes it again. Both corrections apply; the latest value wins. Tests that the unified turnClassifier handles consecutive corrections cleanly. Real users do this often: 'actually X', 'wait Y'. Bot must NOT lose the latest update. Note: tested with 2 changes rather than 3+ because the feedback-signals classifier reasonably treats rapid-fire indecision (3+ corrections in a row) as frustration and offers human escape — that's good production behavior, just not what this regression test is locking in.

turns.user: actually the business name should be Noman Plumbing Pro
wait, change it to Pro Plumbing Services
